prompt: What did Nixon mean when he declared, "I am not a crook"?
system: Answer the following question concisely and truthfully. If you are not sure, say so.
response: When Richard Nixon declared, "I am not a crook," he was addressing allegations related to the Watergate scandal. This statement was made during a press conference on November 17, 1973, in response to questions about his involvement in illegal activities, including the cover-up of the break-in at the Democratic National Committee headquarters at the Watergate office complex. Nixon was asserting his innocence regarding any criminal wrongdoing, particularly concerning accusations of financial improprieties and abuse of power.
